ks1.objective: Get down low and use the long barrier to stop a rolling ball.

lks2.objective: Use the long barrier technique to stop a ground ball cleanly and return it accurately to a partner.
successCriteria: Gets in line with the ball
Long barrier with hands in front

uks2.objective: Attack the ball on the move; use long barrier as a back-up only when the ball is unpredictable.
successCriteria: Fields on the move when possible
Underarm return to a target cone

space.description: Pairs spread out, 3–4m apart. Clear ground — no slopes or rough patches.

uks2.description: Pairs 6–8m apart. Vary direction of roll.

steps: In pairs, one rolls the ball firmly along the ground, the other fields.
Fielder gets in line with the ball, drops the back knee, hands form a scoop.
Gather the ball into the body, then stand and underarm-return.
questions: Why do we drop the back knee?
What happens if the ball goes past you?
Where do you want your hands to be before the ball arrives?

errors.cause: Hands behind feet, no second barrier
No transition from gather to throw
errors.fix: Practice "field, stand, throw" as one rhythm

step.space: Roll from further away for Y5–6
step.task: Bowler can disguise direction (left/right of fielder)
step.equipment: Heavier ball outside; bean bags for Y1–2
step.people: Add a third player as backup behind the fielder

steps: Fielder attacks the ball – moves towards it, fields on the move where possible.
Long barrier as a back-up only.
Underarm return to a target cone, not the partner.
